evals.prompt: Design a dense operations dashboard with KPI cards, a filterable table, an empty state, loading skeletons, error recovery, and light/dark themes using the existing design tokens.
evals.expected_output: A clear, accessible product UI specification or implementation that prioritizes scanning, state coverage, responsive behavior, and existing tokens.
expectations: Uses existing tokens and component patterns instead of introducing a second design system
Covers light and dark modes for surfaces, text, borders, charts, and interactive states
Defines loading, empty, error, and success states
Includes keyboard, focus, contrast, and responsive considerations

evals.prompt: Review this mobile-width web form: labels disappear into placeholders, the submit button has no focus style, validation is color-only, and the dialog cannot be closed with Escape.
evals.expected_output: An accessibility and responsive review identifying labeling, focus, error communication, and keyboard-dialog failures with practical corrections.
expectations: Requires persistent programmatic labels rather than placeholder-only labeling
Requires validation text or semantics in addition to color
Requires expected dialog keyboard behavior including Escape
